ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tickets" (
  "ticket_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ticket */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the support ticket */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the issue */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the ticket (e.g., open, closed) */,
  "creator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the ticket */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the support staff assigned to the ticket */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the ticket was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the ticket was last updated */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the ticket (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department related to the ticket */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the issue (e.g., hardware, software, network) */,
  "resolution_time" INTEGER /* Time taken to resolve the ticket */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the ticket */,
  "closed_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the ticket was closed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ticket_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tickets_creator_id FOREIGN KEY ("creator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tickets_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES support_staff ("staff_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., employee, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "active_status" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "support_staff" (
  "staff_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each support staff member */,
  "staff_name" TEXT /* Full name of the support staff member */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the staff member */,
  "online_status" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the staff member is currently online */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the support staff belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the support staff */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the support staff logged in */,
  "active_status" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the support staff account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("staff_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "commands" (
  "command_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each command */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the command used to perform an action */,
  "security_level" INTEGER /* Security level required to execute the command */,
  "help_text" TEXT /* Detailed help text describing the command usage */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the command (e.g., ticket management, user management) */,
  "last_used" TEXT /* Last time the command was used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("command_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ticket_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history record */,
  "ticket_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ticket to which this history entry belongs */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken (e.g., created, closed) */,
  "action_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user or staff member who performed the action */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was performed */,
  "details" TEXT /* Detailed information about the action taken */,
  "resolution_time" INTEGER /* Time taken to resolve the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ticket_history_action_by FOREIGN KEY ("action_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ticket_history_ticket_id FOREIGN KEY ("ticket_id") REFERENCES tickets ("ticket_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "ticket_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ticket related to the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., ticket update, assignment) */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the notification has been read by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_ticket_id FOREIGN KEY ("ticket_id") REFERENCES tickets ("ticket_id")
)
